questions.text: Which of the following carbides is decomposed by water to form CH4 gas?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Beryllium carbide does not decompose with water to form CH4 gas. It is generally less reactive compared to calcium carbide.

Calcium carbide reacts with water to produce acetylene gas (C2H2), not methane (CH4). However, acetylene can undergo further reactions to produce methane, but the direct decomposition does not yield methane.
Magnesium carbide does not readily decompose in water to form CH4 gas. It reacts with water to produce methane, but the reaction is not as efficient as with calcium carbide.
Strontium carbide does not decompose in water to form CH4 gas. It reacts with water to produce acetylene gas, similar to calcium carbide.

questions.explanation: Beryllium carbide does not decompose with water to form CH4 gas. It is generally less reactive compared to calcium carbide.

questions.text: Hl is a stronger base. It reduces H2SO4 to:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Sulfuric acid does not reduce to elemental sulfur (S) under normal conditions.
Sulfuric acid does not reduce to sulfur dioxide (SO2) under normal conditions.
Sulfurous acid (H2SO3) is not produced directly in the reduction of sulfuric acid (H2SO4) by hydroiodic acid (HI).

In the reduction of sulfuric acid (H2SO4) by hydroiodic acid (HI), sulfuric acid undergoes reduction, producing hydrogen sulfide gas (H2S). The reaction can be represented as:2HI + H2SO4 → 2H2O + H2S + I2Here, sulfur in sulfuric acid is reduced from an oxidation state of +6 to -2, forming hydrogen sulfide gas (H2S).

questions.explanation: H2S is correct because it is the product formed when Hl reduces H2SO4 due to the reduction of sulfur in sulfuric acid.

questions.text: Which of the following will form yellow coloured complex compound?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Scandium (Sc+3) typically forms colorless or white compounds and does not commonly form colored complex compounds.
Copper (I) compounds can form colored complex compounds, but the color is usually not yellow. Copper (I) complexes often exhibit colors like red, orange, or brown depending on the ligands involved.
Iron (III) ions can form yellow colored complex compounds with certain ligands. For example, the complex [Fe(CN)6]3- (hexacyanoferrate(III)) is yellow in color.
Zinc (Zn) typically forms colorless or white compounds and does not commonly form colored complex compounds.

questions.explanation: Fe+3 is correct because iron (III) ions can form yellow colored complex compounds under certain conditions.

questions.text: Geometry of [Pt(NH3)4}2+ is square planar, Hybridization of Pt in the complex ion is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This hybridization involves four hybrid orbitals formed by the combination of one s orbital and three p orbitals. It corresponds to a tetrahedral geometry, not square planar.

This hybridization involves the mixing of one s orbital, one d orbital, and two p orbitals to form four hybrid orbitals. While dsp2 hybridization is common for square planar complexes, the coordination number in this case is 4, not 6.

This hybridization involves the mixing of one s orbital, three d orbitals, and one p orbital to form six hybrid orbitals. The resulting hybrid orbitals are used by Pt to form bonds with the four NH3 ligands and achieve the square planar geometry

This hybridization involves the mixing of one s orbital, two d orbitals, and three p orbitals to form six hybrid orbitals.While dsp3 hybridization is common for octahedral complexes with a coordination number of 6, it does not match the geometry of the given complex, which is square planar.

questions.explanation: d2sp3 is correct because it corresponds to the hybridization of Pt in the square planar complex [Pt(NH3)4]2+.

questions.text: In destructive distillation, coal is heated at 500-1000°C in the absence of air. It gives:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: It is correct because it accurately represents the main products obtained from the destructive distillation of coal.

Methane is a component of coal gas and can be produced during destructive distillation, but it is not the only product.Coal itself is not a product of destructive distillation but the starting material.Coaltar is a product of destructive distillation, but this option does not include coal gas, which is also a significant product.
While carbon monoxide, methane, and hydrogen gas are components of coal gas produced during destructive distillation, this option does not include coke or coaltar, which are also important products.

Phenol is a compound that can be obtained from coaltar, but it is not the only product of destructive distillation.Coke and hydrogen gas are products of destructive distillation, but this option does not include coaltar or coal gas.

questions.explanation: Option A is correct because it accurately represents the main products obtained from the destructive distillation of coal.

questions.text: Identify the compound which shows geometrical isomerism:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This compound can't exhibit geometrical iomerism as it doesn't contain a double bond.
This compound is 3-hexene. It shows geometrical iomerism as it contains two different groups attached to the carbons of double bond on either side.
This compound doesn't contain a double bond. Hence, it can't show geometrical iomerism.
This compound also does not fulfill the conditions required for a compound to exhibit geometrical iomerism.

questions.explanation: This compound is 3-hexene. It shows geometrical iomerism as it contains two different groups attached to the carbons of double bond on either side.

questions.text: Which of the following is meta directing group in the electrophilic substitution reactions of monosubstituted benzene?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The amino group is ortho- and para-directing due to its electron-donating nature. It increases electron density at the ortho and para positions, making those positions more reactive towards electrophilic attack.

The methoxy group is also ortho- and para-directing, but to a lesser extent compared to the amino group. It has a weak electron-donating effect via resonance, making the ortho and para positions more reactive.

The sulfonic acid group is meta-directing due to its strong electron-withdrawing nature. It withdraws electron density from the benzene ring through inductive and resonance effects, making the meta position more reactive towards electrophilic attack.

The hydroxyl group is ortho- and para-directing due to its electron-donating nature through resonance. It increases electron density at the ortho and para positions, making those positions more reactive.

questions.explanation: -SO3H is correct because the sulfonic acid group is meta-directing in electrophilic substitution reactions of monosubstituted benzene due to its strong electron-withdrawing effect.

questions.text: Which of the following base will favour substitution reaction as compared to the elimination reaction?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Hydroxide ion is a strong base and favors elimination reactions over substitution reactions. It abstracts protons from the substrate, promoting elimination reactions to form alkenes.
Alkoxide ions are strong bases, similar to hydroxide ion, and also favor elimination reactions over substitution reactions. They abstract protons, leading to elimination to form alkenes.

Amines, such as NH2, are weaker bases compared to hydroxide ion and alkoxide ions. They are less likely to abstract protons from the substrate, thus favoring substitution reactions over elimination reactions.

Iodide ion is a weak base and does not abstract protons as effectively as hydroxide ion or alkoxide ions. However, it may still promote elimination reactions to some extent, especially under certain conditions.

questions.explanation: NH2 (amine) is correct because it is a weaker base compared to hydroxide ion and alkoxide ions, favoring substitution reactions over elimination reactions.

questions.text: Predict the product of reaction:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Methylamine is a primary amine, but it is not the typical product of the reduction of acetamides. While primary amines can be formed in this reaction, the specific product of acetamide reduction is ethylamine, not methylamine.

Ethylamine is the correct product of the reduction of acetamides. Acetamides, when reduced, undergo cleavage of the amide bond to form a primary amine. In this case, the specific primary amine formed is ethylamine.
2-Propanol is not the product of the reduction of acetamides. The reduction of acetamides leads to the formation of primary amines, not alcohols.
It is not the product of the reduction of acetamides. The reduction of acetamides leads to the formation of primary amines, not carboxylic acids.

questions.explanation: Ethylamine is the correct product of the reduction of acetamides. Acetamides, when reduced, undergo cleavage of the amide bond to form a primary amine. In this case, the specific primary amine formed is ethylamine.

questions.text: Which of the following alcohols on reaction with K2Cr2O7 I H2SO4 will give an alkene?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Primary alcohols undergo oxidation to form aldehydes first and then can be further oxidized to carboxylic acids. They do not directly give alkenes under these conditions.
Secondary alcohols undergo oxidation to form ketones. They also do not directly give alkenes under these conditions.

Tertiary alcohols do not undergo oxidation with potassium dichromate and sulfuric acid to form alkenes. Tertiary alcohols are resistant to oxidation because they lack a hydrogen atom attached to the carbon bearing the hydroxyl group, which is necessary for the oxidation reaction to occur.

questions.explanation: Tertiary alcohols do not undergo oxidation with potassium dichromate and sulfuric acid to form alkenes. Tertiary alcohols are resistant to oxidation because they lack a hydrogen atom attached to the carbon bearing the hydroxyl group, which is necessary for the oxidation reaction to occur.

questions.text: Oxidative cleavage of Ethane-1, 2-diol (Glycol) with HIO4 gives:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: When ethane-1,2-diol undergoes oxidative cleavage, it forms two molecules of formaldehyde. This is because the carbon-carbon bond cleavage results in two aldehyde groups.

Acetaldehyde is not formed in this reaction. Acetaldehyde has one carbon atom bonded to two hydrogen atoms and a carbonyl group. There's no mechanism in the oxidative cleavage of ethane-1,2-diol to produce acetaldehyde.
Methanol is not formed in this reaction. Methanol contains a single hydroxyl group attached to a carbon atom. There's no formation of methanol through oxidative cleavage of ethane-1,2-diol.
Ethanol is not formed in this reaction. Ethanol has a hydroxyl group attached to a two-carbon chain. There's no formation of ethanol through oxidative cleavage of ethane-1,2-diol.

questions.explanation: Formaldehyde (CHO): When ethane-1,2-diol undergoes oxidative cleavage, it forms two molecules of formaldehyde. This is because the carbon-carbon bond cleavage results in two aldehyde groups.

questions.text: Which of the following tests is applied to differentiate between ethanal and propanal?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This reaction involves the self-condensation of aldehydes or ketones to form β-hydroxyaldehydes or β-hydroxyketones, respectively. It doesn't specifically differentiate between ethanal and propanal.
Cannizzaro’s reaction is a disproportionation reaction of aldehydes lacking alpha hydrogens. It doesn't differentiate between ethanal and propanal.

This test is used to distinguish between methyl ketones (like propanone) and aldehydes containing a methyl group adjacent to the carbonyl group (like ethanol). Ethanal can undergo the iodoform reaction to give a yellow precipitate of iodoform (CHI₃), while propanal does not react with the iodoform test.

The Lucas test is used to differentiate between primary, secondary, and tertiary alcohols based on their reaction rates with Lucas reagent (concentrated HCl and ZnCl₂). It doesn't differentiate between ethanal and propanal.

questions.explanation: Iodoform test, as it specifically distinguishes between ethanal and propanal by their reactions with iodine and hydroxide ions to form or not form iodoform precipitate.

questions.text: Acetic acid is reacted with NH3, and resulting compound is strongly heated. Identify the compound formed?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This is the structure of methylamine, which is not the expected product of the reaction between acetic acid and ammonia.
This option is incorrect because it doesn't represent any compound formed from the reaction.
This is acetamide, the correct product of the reaction between acetic acid and ammonia. The carboxylic acid group (-COOH) of acetic acid reacts with ammonia to form an amide bond (-CONH2).
This structure represents acetonitrile, which is not formed in the reaction between acetic acid and ammonia.

questions.explanation: Option c is correct because it corresponds to the formation of acetamide through the reaction between acetic acid and ammonia, leading to the replacement of the carboxylic acid group with an amide group.

questions.text: Which of the following nitrogenous bases is NOT present in DNA?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Guanine is one of the four nitrogenous bases found in DNA, pairing with cytosine via three hydrogen bonds.
Cytosine is also one of the four nitrogenous bases present in DNA, pairing with guanine via three hydrogen bonds.
Thymine is another nitrogenous base found in DNA, pairing with adenine via two hydrogen bonds.
Uracil is not typically found in DNA. Instead, it is present in RNA as a complementary base to adenine, forming RNA-DNA hybrids during processes like transcription.

questions.explanation: Uracil is not typically found in DNA. Instead, it is present in RNA as a complementary base to adenine, forming RNA-DNA hybrids during processes like transcription.

questions.text: Nylon-6,6 is the polymer of:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Nylon-6,6 is a polymer formed by the condensation polymerization of hexamethylene diamine and adipic acid. Hexamethylene diamine provides the amine functional group (-NH2), and adipic acid provides the carboxylic acid functional group (-COOH). These functional groups react to form amide bonds (-CONH-) in a condensation reaction, creating the repeating unit of nylon-6,6.

This combination does not produce nylon-6,6. Ethylene glycol and phthalic acid are used to produce another type of nylon, specifically nylon-6, which is formed through the polymerization of caprolactam, not phthalic acid.

This combination does not produce nylon-6,6 either. It's similar to option A, but ethylene glycol is not used in the production of nylon-6,6. Instead, it's used in the production of another type of nylon called nylon-6, which is formed through the polymerization of caprolactam.
Phthalic acid is not typically used in the production of nylon polymers. Instead, adipic acid is the preferred dicarboxylic acid used alongside hexamethylene diamine to produce nylon-6,6.

questions.explanation: Nylon-6,6 is a polymer formed by the condensation polymerization of hexamethylene diamine and adipic acid. Hexamethylene diamine provides the amine functional group (-NH2), and adipic acid provides the carboxylic acid functional group (-COOH). These functional groups react to form amide bonds (-CONH-) in a condensation reaction, creating the repeating unit of nylon-6,6.

questions.text: Identify greenhouse gases:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: While methane (CH4) is indeed a greenhouse gas, oxygen (O2) is not considered a greenhouse gas. Greenhouse gases trap heat in the Earth's atmosphere, and oxygen does not have this property.

Carbon dioxide (CO2) is a well-known greenhouse gas that contributes to global warming by trapping heat in the atmosphere. Water vapor (H2O) is also a potent greenhouse gas. While its concentration in the atmosphere varies greatly depending on factors such as temperature and humidity, it plays a significant role in the Earth's greenhouse effect.

Nitrogen dioxide (NO2) is an air pollutant and a component of smog, but it is not a significant greenhouse gas. While water vapor (H2O) is indeed a greenhouse gas, NO2 does not contribute significantly to the greenhouse effect compared to other gases like CO2 and methane.

Methane (CH4) is a potent greenhouse gas, but ozone (O3) is not considered a major greenhouse gas. While ozone is important in the Earth's atmosphere as it absorbs ultraviolet radiation from the Sun, it does not have the same significant contribution to the greenhouse effect as CO2 or water vapor.

questions.explanation: This is correct as it includes both carbon dioxide (CO2) and water vapor (H2O), which are major greenhouse gases contributing to the greenhouse effect on Earth.

questions.text: In IR spectrum, a strong peak at 1720 cm-1 indicates the presence of:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: A strong peak at 1720 cm-1 indicates the presence of a C=O bond, not a C=C bond. This is because the stretching vibration of the carbonyl group (C=O) typically occurs in this region.
The carbonyl group (C=O) exhibits a strong absorption band around 1720 cm-1 due to the stretching vibration of the double bond between the carbon and oxygen atoms.
This bond usually appears in the region of 2200-2260 cm-1, not at 1720 cm-1.
This bond typically appears at a lower wavenumber, around 3300-3600 cm-1, due to the stretching vibration of the O-H bond.

questions.explanation: The carbonyl group (C=O) exhibits a strong absorption band around 1720 cm-1 due to the stretching vibration of the double bond between the carbon and oxygen atoms.
